name: Ozik
classification_notes: Ozik is a headline display face with rounded counters and clean-cut terminals drawn from album lettering, placing it firmly in the geometric display category rather than any text-oriented classification.
summary: A four-weight display face with rounded counters and clean-cut terminals, drawn from the lettering on Black Sabbath's Vol. 4 album cover.
description: Built for large settings, Ozik takes its cues from the iconic lettering on Black Sabbath's Vol. 4 record, translating that heavy-metal graphic energy into a usable type family. The four weights run from Regular through to Black, each sharing the same signature rounded counters and crisp terminals that give the face its slightly damaged, geometric character. Best tracked tight and set large in editorial, poster or identity work where a controlled touch of metal is the brief.
foundry_blurb: Nuform Type describe Ozik as a four-weight display face paying homage to the lettering on Black Sabbath's Vol. 4 album. They recommend setting it large and tracking it tight or loose, noting that its rounded counters and clean-cut terminals will give any design a lil' touch of metal.

name: OZIK Soft
classification_notes: OZIK Soft is a rounded geometric sans-serif with circular construction and soft terminals, derived from the geometric OZIK. Its rounded edges and welcoming curves place it firmly in the display category with geometric origins.
summary: A rounded offshoot of Nuform Type's geometric OZIK, softening its sharp edges into warm, circular terminals inspired partly by VAG Rounded.
description: Built from the same circular geometry as OZIK, this variant replaces sharp details with rounded terminals across a range of weights from Regular to Black. The rounding process required significant redrawing, including adjustments to letter width, diagonal strokes and select letterforms that diverged from the original to preserve a distinct identity. Stylistic alternates for 'a', 'e' and 'g' are included to address legibility at smaller sizes.
foundry_blurb: Nuform Type describe OZIK Soft as a friendly rounded font inspired by the original OZIK, retaining its predecessor's charm through rounded edges and welcoming curves. The design process involved multiple rounds of revision to avoid the narrowing effect that early rounding attempts introduced, and drew inspiration from VAG Rounded's balanced proportions and distinctive rounded terminals.

name: Roko
classification_notes: Roko is explicitly a display typeface drawn in the spirit of the Lubalin/Carnase geometric sans tradition, with tight letterspacing and personality-driven letterforms intended for large display settings.
summary: A five-weight geometric display sans drawn in the spirit of Lubalin and Carnase, designed to be set tight at large sizes.
description: Roko takes its cue from Herb Lubalin's Afrodite logo and the tightly letterspaced geo-sans typography of that era, updated with just enough personality to feel fresh. The family runs five weights from an ultra-light intended only for massive display use through to a more practical bold, with the now-characteristic hugging double-r as its distinguishing detail. Intended for display setting where tight spacing and a hint of retro warmth are welcome.
foundry_blurb: Nuform Type describe Roko as a five-weight display typeface that loves to be set tight and party late, drawn in the spirit of the tightly letterspaced Lubalin and Carnase geometric sans era with an updated touch. Its spark was the Afrodite logo by Herb Lubalin, which originally inspired the Nuform Type logo before the positive reaction prompted them to develop it into a full typeface.

name: Nuform Sans
classification_notes: The clean, even strokes and functional character suggest a geometric or neo-grotesque sans, positioned as a dependable workhorse companion to a display typeface.
summary: A five-weight sans-serif designed as the text companion to Nuform Type's display face OZIK, built for reliable everyday use.
description: A functional sans family in five weights from Regular to Bold, designed to pair with the display typeface OZIK. The family is positioned as a versatile workhorse, equally capable of supporting display use or handling body text with understated character. The limited weight range and absence of italics keeps it lean and focused.
foundry_blurb: Nuform Type describe Nuform Sans as a dependable five-weight family designed to complement its display counterpart OZIK. They characterise it as capable of both leading and supporting, able to take centre stage or assist in any context through subtle charm and reliability.

name: Hermanos
classification_notes: Hermanos is a display typeface with flared serifs and a narrow stature, directly inspired by hand-painted signage from San Francisco's Mission District, placing it firmly in the Latin display tradition.
summary: A narrow display face with flared serifs drawn from the hand-painted sign tradition of San Francisco's Mission District.
description: Rooted in the painted lettering culture of the Mission District, Hermanos carries the informal warmth of hand-made signage into a structured typeface. Its narrow proportions and flared serifs give it a cheerful, slightly ornate character that suits packaging, restaurant branding and seasonal display work. The Chicano lettering vernacular that informs it gives the face a specificity of reference rarely found in display type.
foundry_blurb: Nuform Type describe Hermanos as a dedication to the hand-painted signs of San Francisco's Mission District, citing its flared serifs, narrow stature and cheerful spirit as making it suited to packaging, restaurants and Halloween headlines.

name: BRZO
classification_notes: A bold italic display sans inspired by 90s sports graphics, designed for headlines and high-impact layouts. Tight letter spacing and intentional slant place it firmly in display rather than text use.
summary: A five-style bold italic display sans drawn from 90s basketball graphics, built for headlines and sports-inflected layouts.
description: Inspired by Dream Team posters, Fleer trading cards and Eastbay catalogues, BRZO is a full-bodied italic display sans with intentionally tight letter spacing for additional punch at headline sizes. The family runs across five styles including Basic, Gloss and Chrome variants, inviting diagonal setting, stacking and drop shadows. It occupies the same visual territory as sports jersey typography and early-90s athletic print design.
foundry_blurb: Nuform Type describe BRZO as a 5-style display typeface inspired by the boldness of 90s basketball graphics, referencing Dream Team posters, Fleer trading cards and Eastbay catalogs as source material. They note that BRZO's intentionally tight letter spacing gives headlines additional punch, and suggest stacking, diagonal setting or drop shadows as ways to deploy it.

name: Jaws
classification_notes: Jaws is explicitly described as a reverse contrast typeface, a display category rooted in broad nib calligraphy that inverts conventional stroke weight logic for expressive, attention-grabbing effect.
summary: A reverse contrast display face drawing loose inspiration from broad nib calligraphy, reframed for contemporary use.
description: Built around inverted stroke contrast, Jaws takes the weight logic of broad nib calligraphy and turns it on its head. The foundry positions it as a contemporary take rather than a revival, shedding its calligraphic origins in favour of something bolder and more direct. Named after the James Bond henchman, the personality leans into brute force rather than elegance.
foundry_blurb: Nuform Type describe Jaws as a reverse contrast typeface unlike any other, named after and inspired by the iconic James Bond henchman. They note it began as a fresh take on broad nib calligraphy before evolving away from its classical roots into something firmly contemporary.
